channel.title: ConnectBlue Political Ad Tech News
channel.description: The latest news on political advertising, ad tech trends, campaigns, and digital strategies for the 2026 midterm elections.

item.title: 83% of Political Consultants Now Use AI—What That Means for Your Campaign Operations
item.description: 83% of political consultants use AI (up 24 points YoY); campaigns deploying chatbots, AI-generated creative, and data analysis. Winning campaigns embed AI in operations (creative, outreach, data); by November, AI infrastructure will be table stakes.
item.content:encoded: 83% of political consultants use AI (up 24 points YoY); campaigns deploying chatbots, AI-generated creative, and data analysis. Winning campaigns embed AI in operations (creative, outreach, data); by November, AI infrastructure will be table stakes.

item.title: Influencers Are Now a $1K+ Budget Line Item for Campaigns—Here's How to Scale It
item.description: First major influencer election: Steyer campaign paid $1K-$100K to creators. FTC/state disclosure rules now mandatory (California, Texas). Micro-influencer networks outperform programmatic display 2-3x on engagement; campaigns should budget 15-20% of digital spend for creator partnerships.
item.content:encoded: First major influencer election: Steyer campaign paid $1K-$100K to creators. FTC/state disclosure rules now mandatory (California, Texas). Micro-influencer networks outperform programmatic display 2-3x on engagement; campaigns should budget 15-20% of digital spend for creator partnerships.

item.title: Real Ads, Fake Images—Michigan AI Disclosure Law Becomes Baseline for Campaigns
item.description: 30 states now regulate AI in political ads; Michigan requires clear disclosure of AI-generated content with criminal penalties (up to 5 years). Campaigns must audit state-by-state AI rules and budget compliance review into creative workflows.

item.title: Publicis and The Trade Desk Settle Dispute—DSP Transparency Matters More Than Ever
item.description: Publicis-Trade Desk dispute resolved after fee-stacking audit; campaigns must audit DSP fee structures and demand explicit margin transparency in all media buys. Holdco pressure on DSP fees is now table stakes.
item.content:encoded: Publicis-Trade Desk dispute resolved after fee-stacking audit; campaigns must audit DSP fee structures and demand explicit margin transparency in all media buys. Holdco pressure on DSP fees is now table stakes.

item.title: Gray Media Forges Advertising Pact with Madhive
item.description: Gray Media (largest local TV broadcaster) partners with Madhive DSP + Maverick AI to integrate voter/political data (TargetSmart, Data Trust, L2, Tunnl). Local broadcasters now have programmatic, data-driven political targeting capabilities previously available only to national DSP buyers. This democratizes precision targeting and amplifies local media's competitive position.
item.content:encoded: Gray Media (largest local TV broadcaster) partners with Madhive DSP + Maverick AI to integrate voter/political data (TargetSmart, Data Trust, L2, Tunnl). Local broadcasters now have programmatic, data-driven political targeting capabilities previously available only to national DSP buyers. This democratizes precision targeting and amplifies local media's competitive position.

item.title: Mediaocean Introduces NIVO AI with Innovid Agents Driving 90% Improvement in Speed to Campaign Launch
item.description: Mediaocean's NIVO AI orchestrates campaign workflow across creative, delivery, and measurement—90% faster setup than manual campaign builds. Specialized AI agents handle creative generation, campaign trafficking, QA, and optimization. This operational breakthrough reduces manual overhead and compresses campaign time-to-live by weeks.

item.title: Fastest Political Ad Spend Through June Surges To A Record $4B
item.description: Political ad spending hits record $4B through June—fastest pace in history. CTV now $2.7B (up $300M), broadcast TV $5.6B (up $400M). Full-year projection raised to $11.6B. CTV is the only channel growing budget share—campaigns without scaled CTV by Q3 face inventory constraints and cost premiums.
item.content:encoded: Political ad spending hits record $4B through June—fastest pace in history. CTV now $2.7B (up $300M), broadcast TV $5.6B (up $400M). Full-year projection raised to $11.6B. CTV is the only channel growing budget share—campaigns without scaled CTV by Q3 face inventory constraints and cost premiums.

item.title: IQM Brings AI to Building Voter Targeting With Custom Voter Audience Tool
item.description: IQM launches AI-powered voter audience builder using natural language interface—campaigns instantly generate precise, activatable segments from multi-source voter data. Eliminates manual segment selection and hours of planning. This is the speed advantage separating winners from laggards in 2026.
item.content:encoded: IQM launches AI-powered voter audience builder using natural language interface—campaigns instantly generate precise, activatable segments from multi-source voter data. Eliminates manual segment selection and hours of planning. This is the speed advantage separating winners from laggards in 2026.

item.title: Surveillance Pricing Crackdown Gains Momentum Across States
item.description: Connecticut and New York advance legislation banning surveillance pricing. States tightening restrictions on personal data use for algorithmic personalization. Privacy regulation wave signals coming restrictions on behavioral and PII targeting in political advertising.
item.content:encoded: Connecticut and New York advance legislation banning surveillance pricing. States tightening restrictions on personal data use for algorithmic personalization. Privacy regulation wave signals coming restrictions on behavioral and PII targeting in political advertising.

item.title: Pew Research 2026 Political Typology Released
item.description: Pew identifies nine voter segments including Tuned-Out Middle: 56% lower income, 19% follow news, 32% voted in 2024. Represents massive untapped persuasion and turnout opportunity requiring different messaging and channels than engaged voters.
item.content:encoded: Pew identifies nine voter segments including Tuned-Out Middle: 56% lower income, 19% follow news, 32% voted in 2024. Represents massive untapped persuasion and turnout opportunity requiring different messaging and channels than engaged voters.

item.title: Amazon DSP Adds Adelaide Attention Targeting for Pre-Bid Optimization
item.description: Real-time attention-based targeting now live in Amazon DSP. Advertisers can filter inventory by viewer engagement levels. Political campaigns still optimizing on reach/frequency waste 15-20% of budget; attention metrics increase ad recall 2-3x. Critical capability gap.
item.content:encoded: Real-time attention-based targeting now live in Amazon DSP. Advertisers can filter inventory by viewer engagement levels. Political campaigns still optimizing on reach/frequency waste 15-20% of budget; attention metrics increase ad recall 2-3x. Critical capability gap.

item.title: ActBlue CEO Repeatedly Invokes Fifth Amendment at Congressional Hearing
item.description: Democratic fundraising infrastructure vulnerabilities exposed. ActBlue CEO invokes Fifth Amendment on foreign donation vetting procedures. Campaigns using platform face new compliance risk and vendor liability. Alternative fundraising platforms gain strategic advantage.

item.title: 7 Programmatic Advertising Trends Shaping 2026: AI Media Quality, Data, CTV, and Curation
item.description: AI-generated content flooding programmatic supply makes media quality a performance issue. 54% of advertisers report quality decline. Data consolidation essential; fragmented data limits AI effectiveness. Campaigns must implement curated supply paths. Generic programmatic buying wastes 20-30% of budget.
item.content:encoded: AI-generated content flooding programmatic supply makes media quality a performance issue. 54% of advertisers report quality decline. Data consolidation essential; fragmented data limits AI effectiveness. Campaigns must implement curated supply paths. Generic programmatic buying wastes 20-30% of budget.

item.title: Oklahoma Ethics Commission Regulates AI-Generated Ads: 36 States Now Have Rules
item.description: Oklahoma campaigns spent $60M on ads with zero rules for AI-generated content. Ethics Commission drafting transparency requirements. 36 states already have AI ad regulations. Campaigns must plan compliance by July. Non-disclosure creates legal and credibility risk by Q4.
item.content:encoded: Oklahoma campaigns spent $60M on ads with zero rules for AI-generated content. Ethics Commission drafting transparency requirements. 36 states already have AI ad regulations. Campaigns must plan compliance by July. Non-disclosure creates legal and credibility risk by Q4.

item.title: Tracking the Flood of AI Political Spending: $125M PAC War on Regulation
item.description: Leading the Future super PAC raised $125M from OpenAI, Andreessen Horowitz, Palantir to oppose AI regulation. Follows crypto 2024 playbook. Campaigns must track funding sources and prepare for pretextual ads. AI PAC spending will reach $500M+ by November.
item.content:encoded: Leading the Future super PAC raised $125M from OpenAI, Andreessen Horowitz, Palantir to oppose AI regulation. Follows crypto 2024 playbook. Campaigns must track funding sources and prepare for pretextual ads. AI PAC spending will reach $500M+ by November.

item.title: Beyond Red and Blue: Resonate Data Reveals How Distinct Voter Factions Are Reshaping 2026 Campaign Strategy
item.description: Four distinct voter factions have completely different priorities and emotional drivers. Generic partisan messaging misses 75% of persuadable voters. Campaigns using precision segmentation will outperform those treating electorate as two blocs in swing districts.
item.content:encoded: Four distinct voter factions have completely different priorities and emotional drivers. Generic partisan messaging misses 75% of persuadable voters. Campaigns using precision segmentation will outperform those treating electorate as two blocs in swing districts.

item.title: Embrace AI – Campaigns Can Better Serve Voters With New Tools
item.description: C&E opinion: AI-powered voter targeting using neuroscience and 'Voter Digital Twins' moves beyond polling to precision messaging. Campaign operatives can use AI for message testing, targeting, and creative generation, freeing strategists for high-level decisions. AI is not a threat to campaign professionals—it's a force multiplier.
item.content:encoded: C&E opinion: AI-powered voter targeting using neuroscience and 'Voter Digital Twins' moves beyond polling to precision messaging. Campaign operatives can use AI for message testing, targeting, and creative generation, freeing strategists for high-level decisions. AI is not a threat to campaign professionals—it's a force multiplier.

item.title: How Campaigns Are Harnessing Social Media During Election Season
item.description: CAP Radio reports: campaigns leveraging influencer partnerships, AI-generated content, and organic-looking endorsements at scale. Disclosure gaps and influencer non-transparency create competitive advantage for early movers but regulatory risk. Social media now drives messaging 50%+ in primaries; campaigns without social strategy are structurally disadvantaged.
item.content:encoded: CAP Radio reports: campaigns leveraging influencer partnerships, AI-generated content, and organic-looking endorsements at scale. Disclosure gaps and influencer non-transparency create competitive advantage for early movers but regulatory risk. Social media now drives messaging 50%+ in primaries; campaigns without social strategy are structurally disadvantaged.

item.title: Media Transparency Is Still Broken: Here's What Marketers Need to Do About It
item.description: MediaPost analysis: programmatic working media only 43.3% transparent after 10 years of industry focus. New opacity risks (walled gardens, agentic AI systems, offshore intermediaries) mean political campaigns need independent financial governance for media spending—not trust-based agency relationships.
item.content:encoded: MediaPost analysis: programmatic working media only 43.3% transparent after 10 years of industry focus. New opacity risks (walled gardens, agentic AI systems, offshore intermediaries) mean political campaigns need independent financial governance for media spending—not trust-based agency relationships.

item.title: PubMatic and Havas Launch First Agentic Campaign in Spain—18% CPM Savings, 23% Higher Impressions
item.description: PubMatic's AgenticOS executes first European agentic CTV campaign for Telefónica: 80% faster setup, 18% CPM efficiency, 23% impression overperformance. Signals the immediate viability of autonomous AI-driven media buying at scale for 2026 political campaigns.
item.content:encoded: PubMatic's AgenticOS executes first European agentic CTV campaign for Telefónica: 80% faster setup, 18% CPM efficiency, 23% impression overperformance. Signals the immediate viability of autonomous AI-driven media buying at scale for 2026 political campaigns.

item.title: Democratic Group Launches $50 Million Midterm Blitz
item.description: American Bridge 21st Century scaling into 12+ House and Senate races with early $50M spend—early-cycle investment signals confidence in targeted reach at scale, directly pressuring opponent campaigns to allocate faster and broader across channels.

item.title: CTV Trends Report: News on Streaming Is Underutilized Due to Brand Safety Concerns—Scene-Level Targeting Unlocks 20+ Points of Safe Inventory
item.description: Scene-level CTV targeting reveals 35.7% of News is fully brand-safe, but campaigns using broad exclusions miss 20-30% of available safe inventory. Early adopters of scene-level targeting gain material CPM advantage until competitors catch up.
item.content:encoded: Scene-level CTV targeting reveals 35.7% of News is fully brand-safe, but campaigns using broad exclusions miss 20-30% of available safe inventory. Early adopters of scene-level targeting gain material CPM advantage until competitors catch up.

item.title: Tech: AI Election Spending Has Been Cautious—PACs Are Not Dominating 2026 Like Crypto Did in 2024
item.description: AI-aligned PACs spending far more cautiously than crypto did in 2024. Traditional campaign structures still dominate; campaigns should focus on traditional fundraising cycles and donor networks, not competing for AI PAC attention.
item.content:encoded: AI-aligned PACs spending far more cautiously than crypto did in 2024. Traditional campaign structures still dominate; campaigns should focus on traditional fundraising cycles and donor networks, not competing for AI PAC attention.

item.title: ADvendio Launches Specialised AI Workforce for Agentic Advertising — Ad Ops Gets 9 New AI Agent Roles
item.description: First agentic AI built specifically for advertising enables autonomous campaign optimization, from proposal generation to real-time budget reallocation. Campaigns deploying autonomous ad ops workflows now will outpace manual media buying processes by Q4 2026.

item.title: Oklahoma Ethics Commission Addresses AI in Political Ads — Transparency Requirements Coming
item.description: AI political ad regulation is crystallizing across 30+ states with disclosure requirements as the consensus standard. Campaigns running AI-generated ads without disclosure infrastructure by July face state-level exposure in fall races.

item.title: Alaska Senate Race Shows Real-Time Ad Spending Asymmetry: Democrats Lead in Airtime by Nearly 2-to-1
item.description: In the Alaska U.S. Senate race, Democrats have already spent $6.4 million on ad airtime compared to Republicans at $3.4 million according to AdImpact data. This 1.9x spend advantage in early June shows how 2026 races are diverging on media investment strategy and targeting approach, providing real-time signals about where major party committees are prioritizing resources.

item.title: OpenAI Activates CPA Bidding in ChatGPT: Discovery Channel Shifts Into Performance Optimization Territory
item.description: OpenAI activated cost-per-action (CPA) bidding in ChatGPT on June 4, 2026, enabling advertisers to pay only when users convert. This structural shift enables conversion-optimized bidding that treats ChatGPT as a high-intent discovery channel, directly competing with traditional search for performance budget.

item.title: Political Ad Infrastructure Faces Major Transparency Gaps: Proxy Spend Loopholes Show 85 Percent Enforcement Difference
item.description: Political ad infrastructure is facing major transparency gaps between officially registered political parties (94% enforcement) and unverified shadow or proxy networks (9% enforcement). This enforcement disparity directly impacts campaign budget allocation and competitive intelligence.

item.title: Video Ad Spending to Set Record for a Midterm Cycle in 2026
item.description: CTV projected to grow $1.3B (16% increase to $2.9B) while linear broadcast flat. Cost per persuadable swing voter hits $516. Campaigns that scale CTV early lock in inventory; late-cycle buyers face 5x CPM premiums and no availability.
item.content:encoded: CTV projected to grow $1.3B (16% increase to $2.9B) while linear broadcast flat. Cost per persuadable swing voter hits $516. Campaigns that scale CTV early lock in inventory; late-cycle buyers face 5x CPM premiums and no availability.

item.title: Archetype Data and Aristotle Announce Partnership to Power Next-Generation Political Audience Segments
item.description: B2B identity linkage + voter data = 360-degree audience view across home and work. Unified targeting reaches swing voters at household level AND professional/business context. Activation across LiveRamp, Trade Desk, Google, other DSPs.

item.title: Voter Intelligence at the Speed of Media: How Aristotle and Always On Data Are Powering the 2026 Cycle
item.description: Real-time voter intelligence + unified DSP activation (Trade Desk, StackAdapt, Google, Yahoo): Campaigns compress 4-week activation cycles to 4-day cycles. Voter intelligence pre-activated across all major platforms eliminates custom pipeline delays.
item.content:encoded: Real-time voter intelligence + unified DSP activation (Trade Desk, StackAdapt, Google, Yahoo): Campaigns compress 4-week activation cycles to 4-day cycles. Voter intelligence pre-activated across all major platforms eliminates custom pipeline delays.

item.title: DSPolitical and FreeWheel Unlock Scalable, High-Performance CTV Ahead of 2026 Midterm Elections
item.description: Deterministic voter data + premium CTV activation: DSPolitical achieves 18x higher match performance, 30% faster audience onboarding. Unified first-party voter data infrastructure now separates competitive winners from laggards.
item.content:encoded: Deterministic voter data + premium CTV activation: DSPolitical achieves 18x higher match performance, 30% faster audience onboarding. Unified first-party voter data infrastructure now separates competitive winners from laggards.

item.title: How Data Powers Modern Political Polling and Messaging
item.description: Modern political polling now incorporates multi-stream data: voter registration, consumer purchasing, digital behavior, and real-time signals. Campaigns using predictive modeling and dynamic message testing measurably outperform static polling. Critical for 2026: Real-time sentiment monitoring + rapid message iteration = faster response to breaking news and opponent moves.

item.title: What The Trade Desk's Investment Strategy Reveals About Ad Tech's Next Era
item.description: The Trade Desk's TD7 arm is backing Hightouch, a data activation platform signaling the future of political ad targeting. Key shift: DSPs are moving beyond identity-based targeting toward AI-powered data activation tools. Political campaigns need unified data activation strategies across DSPs—not fragmented identity silos.

item.title: By the Numbers: Liftoff's IPO Tests Ad Tech's Mettle on the Public Markets
item.description: Liftoff Mobile raises $437M in first major ad tech IPO since May 2025, signaling investor confidence in AI-powered programmatic platforms. Key implication: Ad tech vendors with 58%+ EBITDA margins and Cortex-like neural networks are attracting capital. Political campaigns using AI-optimized DSPs will see measurable efficiency gains vs. non-AI platforms.
item.content:encoded: Liftoff Mobile raises $437M in first major ad tech IPO since May 2025, signaling investor confidence in AI-powered programmatic platforms. Key implication: Ad tech vendors with 58%+ EBITDA margins and Cortex-like neural networks are attracting capital. Political campaigns using AI-optimized DSPs will see measurable efficiency gains vs. non-AI platforms.

item.title: US Political Ad Spending 2026: Most Expensive Midterm Election Cycle
item.description: Political ad spending in 2026 will exceed $11 billion—the most expensive midterm cycle ever. Critical shift: CTV is now the ONLY growing channel. Campaigns that haven't scaled streaming video by Q3 will face inventory constraints and cost premiums that directly impact swing district reach.
item.content:encoded: Political ad spending in 2026 will exceed $11 billion—the most expensive midterm cycle ever. Critical shift: CTV is now the ONLY growing channel. Campaigns that haven't scaled streaming video by Q3 will face inventory constraints and cost premiums that directly impact swing district reach.

item.title: New Poll Shows Massive Cross-Party Support for Ending Dark Money
item.description: Bipartisan supermajority of voters (70%+) now support ending dark money and restoring campaign finance limits. This voter demand is shifting political calculus around super PAC structure, spending disclosure, and rapid response media—creating opportunity for campaigns positioned as 'transparent' advertisers.

item.title: South Carolina Attorney General Alan Wilson Calls for Rules on AI Political Ads
item.description: South Carolina's AG is pushing for AI disclosure rules on political advertising—requiring campaigns to label AI-generated content. This is the first state-level push for AI advertising transparency that directly impacts 2026 campaigns running synthetic media at scale.

item.title: Digital Remedy Expands Political Media Offering With Launch Of The Political Desk Ahead of 2026 Midterms
item.description: Digital Remedy launched 'The Political Desk'—a unified platform combining media planning tools, real-time analytics, and measurement capabilities for political campaigns. Platform integrates linear, CTV, digital, and programmatic in a single interface, addressing the fragmentation crisis in political media buying.

item.title: PharosGraph and AdImpact Partner to Deliver Real-Time Narrative and Competitive Intelligence for Political Advertising
item.description: AdImpact and PharosGraph unified ad tracking with narrative intelligence—campaigns now see real-time correlation between messaging spend and voter sentiment shifts. AdScape integrates hyperlocal narrative data across DSPs, enabling message-to-outcome measurement in hours instead of weeks.

item.title: Tech 'got spanked' in this week's primaries. It could be a preview of more to come
item.description: Tech spending and endorsements failed dramatically across primary races—a strategic signal that traditional media + grassroots mobilization outpaced venture-backed candidate campaigns. For 2026 midterms, this signals a fundamental shift in campaign spending priorities away from tech-enabled approaches.
item.content:encoded: Tech spending and endorsements failed dramatically across primary races—a strategic signal that traditional media + grassroots mobilization outpaced venture-backed candidate campaigns. For 2026 midterms, this signals a fundamental shift in campaign spending priorities away from tech-enabled approaches.

item.title: What Meta, Google and TikTok's ads MCP servers mean for media buyers
item.description: Meta, Google, TikTok, Amazon now offer MCP (Model Context Protocol) servers for ads. AI agents (Claude, ChatGPT) can manage campaigns across all platforms via single interface. Massive operational advantage: unified activation, fewer errors, faster optimization. Campaigns without AI agent integration will lag on execution speed and cross-platform coordination.

item.title: Vulnerable members spend big on official comms
item.description: Competitive House members spending $380K+ on taxpayer-funded official communications (franking) in Q4 2025 and Q1 2026. Rep. April McClain Delaney (D-MD) tops list; practice blurs lines between official and campaign activity. Spending surge signals competitive primary/general races; campaigns must monitor and counter official messaging.
item.content:encoded: Competitive House members spending $380K+ on taxpayer-funded official communications (franking) in Q4 2025 and Q1 2026. Rep. April McClain Delaney (D-MD) tops list; practice blurs lines between official and campaign activity. Spending surge signals competitive primary/general races; campaigns must monitor and counter official messaging.

item.title: Tech billionaires are spending unprecedented sums in California races. Experts say it's the tip of the iceberg
item.description: Tech industry pours $200M+ into 2026 California elections: Sergey Brin $82M (billionaire tax), Chris Larsen $26M (via multiple Super PACs targeting legislative races). Fragmented strategy across governor, state legislature, local races. Campaigns competing in California face unprecedented saturation; sophisticated micro-targeting and regional messaging now critical to cut through.
item.content:encoded: Tech industry pours $200M+ into 2026 California elections: Sergey Brin $82M (billionaire tax), Chris Larsen $26M (via multiple Super PACs targeting legislative races). Fragmented strategy across governor, state legislature, local races. Campaigns competing in California face unprecedented saturation; sophisticated micro-targeting and regional messaging now critical to cut through.

item.title: Koch-aligned PAC launches first big Senate ad buy
item.description: Americans for Prosperity Action (AFP) opens with $6.3M across six Senate battlegrounds (MI, OH, IA, NC, NH, MT). Early ad spend reveals strategic priorities: cost-of-living messaging dominates; even Trump-won states (MT, OH) seeing heavy investment. Campaigns must re-evaluate spend allocation by Q2.
item.content:encoded: Americans for Prosperity Action (AFP) opens with $6.3M across six Senate battlegrounds (MI, OH, IA, NC, NH, MT). Early ad spend reveals strategic priorities: cost-of-living messaging dominates; even Trump-won states (MT, OH) seeing heavy investment. Campaigns must re-evaluate spend allocation by Q2.

item.title: Perion Launches New Advanced Features for Programmatic Digital Out of Home
item.description: Programmatic DOOH becomes AI-native with agentic planning, guaranteed delivery, and integrated measurement. 75-80% of DOOH spend still booked direct; Perion brings it into unified programmatic infrastructure—critical advantage for campaigns scaling reach by November 2026.
item.content:encoded: Programmatic DOOH becomes AI-native with agentic planning, guaranteed delivery, and integrated measurement. 75-80% of DOOH spend still booked direct; Perion brings it into unified programmatic infrastructure—critical advantage for campaigns scaling reach by November 2026.

item.title: Millions Pour Into Nevada Primary Ads. A Third of the Money is Untraceable.
item.description: Dark money groups now control $5M+ in Nevada primary ad spend—more than 33% of total airtime budget is untraceable. Service First Fund (Lombardo-affiliated) dominates with $4M spend. Campaigns face unprecedented transparency gaps in swing state ad markets.

item.title: OpenAI Races To Add Conversion-Optimized Campaigns
item.description: OpenAI launches CPA pricing for ChatGPT Ads—campaigns can now optimize conversions directly on the platform with real-time tracking. New revenue channel opens for political advertisers seeking AI-native targeting capabilities.
item.content:encoded: OpenAI launches CPA pricing for ChatGPT Ads—campaigns can now optimize conversions directly on the platform with real-time tracking. New revenue channel opens for political advertisers seeking AI-native targeting capabilities.

item.title: Are these AI-generated ads slamming Jennifer Jenkins and Jared Moskowitz the future of campaigning?
item.description: Republican Party of Florida produced AI-generated attack ads in 2 days at minimal cost, vs. traditional ads taking weeks. Spencer Pratt's AI-powered LA mayor campaign reached 22% support tied with incumbent. Production speed advantage shifts power to campaigns with AI content capability—deniable attribution and regulatory gaps create unfair competitive advantage.
item.content:encoded: Republican Party of Florida produced AI-generated attack ads in 2 days at minimal cost, vs. traditional ads taking weeks. Spencer Pratt's AI-powered LA mayor campaign reached 22% support tied with incumbent. Production speed advantage shifts power to campaigns with AI content capability—deniable attribution and regulatory gaps create unfair competitive advantage.

item.title: Election threats are focused on campaign systems, not voting machines
item.description: Check Point reports 2026 election threats target campaign email, fundraising platforms, and donor databases—not voting infrastructure. 82% of attacks arrive via email; ActBlue and WinRed password compromises suggest attackers are staging follow-on phishing campaigns against campaign staff. Campaign data breaches will measurably impact Q4 messaging by November.

item.title: Political Ad Spending Nears $4 Billion As California Governor's Race Sets Record
item.description: AdImpact reports $3.9B spent through May with $10.8B projected for full cycle— 47% above 4 years ago. California gubernatorial race alone accounts for $315M, with Tom Steyer spending $201M (two-thirds of state total). Late-cycle media buyers will face 15-25% cost premiums in saturated markets.
item.content:encoded: AdImpact reports $3.9B spent through May with $10.8B projected for full cycle— 47% above 4 years ago. California gubernatorial race alone accounts for $315M, with Tom Steyer spending $201M (two-thirds of state total). Late-cycle media buyers will face 15-25% cost premiums in saturated markets.

item.title: OpenAI, AP Political Data-Licensing Deal Signals Possible New Service
item.description: OpenAI and Associated Press expanded their data licensing deal through 2029 to integrate live election data directly into ChatGPT. This signals a fundamental shift: AI models will become primary distribution channels for political information, enabling new voter intelligence and targeting capabilities for campaigns.
item.content:encoded: OpenAI and Associated Press expanded their data licensing deal through 2029 to integrate live election data directly into ChatGPT. This signals a fundamental shift: AI models will become primary distribution channels for political information, enabling new voter intelligence and targeting capabilities for campaigns.

item.title: California is drowning in internet campaign 'slop.' 2028 is next.
item.description: AI-generated ads and influencer payments are flooding California primaries—with 2-day production timelines replacing traditional weeks-long processes. Campaigns without AI content capability face structural disadvantage by Q3 2026.
item.content:encoded: AI-generated ads and influencer payments are flooding California primaries—with 2-day production timelines replacing traditional weeks-long processes. Campaigns without AI content capability face structural disadvantage by Q3 2026.

item.title: Texas population boom, Latino backlash scramble 2026 Senate race
item.description: 2.5M new Texas residents since 2020 with unpredictable voting; Latino disapproval of Trump at 67% (vs 55% support in 2024). Campaigns must rebuild voter models immediately--old data will waste 20-30% of media spend on dead audiences.

item.title: LA mayors race: AI videos supporting Spencer Pratt shake up election
item.description: Viral AI-generated videos created by supporters reach millions organically. Campaigns can leverage user-generated synthetic media without formal spend--grassroots AI content at scale is outperforming traditional paid media in 2026.

item.title: Powerful A.I. Super PACs Duel Over the Midterms: This Is a War
item.description: Competing AI-backed super PACs (Anthropic vs OpenAI) now spend millions to influence 2026 elections. Campaigns must understand AI companies as new power players with policy agendas--vendor consolidation creates asymmetric advantages for aligned campaigns.

item.title: Late surge in CA-48 Super PAC money spend against Campa-Najjar
item.description: Over 3.4M in independent expenditure spending floods CA-48 race
item.content:encoded: Over 3.4M in independent expenditure spending floods CA-48 race

item.title: New ad campaign hits Dems on crypto bill
item.description: New Democracy executes six-figure multi-channel media buy in 11 swing states
item.content:encoded: New Democracy executes six-figure multi-channel media buy in 11 swing states

item.title: USPS seeks mail-in voter lists from states after Trump order
item.description: Federal USPS requirement for centralized voter data creates new compliance implications for campaigns.

item.title: OpenX Powers Political Reach With Preferred Access to Quality Direct Media for 2026 Midterms
item.description: OpenX and Givsly partnership unlocks values-based voter targeting across CTV with ZIP-code precision in under 24 hours. Industry-first: campaigns match voter values against 500+ nonprofit behavioral data. CTV activation speed increases 10x, costs drop for down-ballot campaigns, and values-based reach becomes alternative to voter file targeting.

item.title: Winning Down-Ballot in 2026
item.description: Down-ballot races now represent 50% of political ad spend and grow faster than federal races. Early-cycle spend arrives 4-6 weeks ahead of historical timelines. CTV is foundational to down-ballot strategy. Campaigns identifying demand early win against competitors using legacy playbooks.

item.title: The Persuasion Gap in Political Advertising
item.description: Campaign targeting is split between mobilization and persuasion. Issue-engaged voters signal higher persuadability. For 2026, campaigns using values-based signals with voter files unlock efficiency gains separating winners from laggards in swing districts.

item.title: Attack ads are reaching record levels in the 2026 race
item.description: Attack ads now comprise 72% of campaign ad budgets in 2026—a record-breaking increase from 2022. This spending surge reflects intensified competitive battles and signals dramatic shifts in campaign strategy and media buying priorities.

item.title: Can AI Improve Trust Between Political Campaigns And Voters?
item.description: Convos, a new platform co-founded by Tom Carroll, is rethinking political SMS communications with AI-powered conversational intelligence. The platform manages voter reply streams, prioritizes interactions, and enables genuine two-way engagement at scale—shifting SMS from broadcast to conversation.

item.title: Inside the Democratic resistance on AI
item.description: Progressive Democrats (Sanders, AOC, Khanna, Warren) are mounting coordinated opposition to AI deployment, data centers, and AI-backed Super PACs. This represents a major policy shift that will directly restrict funding mechanisms and technology choices for 2026 campaigns.

item.title: Powerful A.I. Super PACs Duel Over the Midterms: 'This Is a War'
item.description: Anthropic-backed and OpenAI-backed Super PACs are spending millions on rival campaign operations in 2026 midterms. This represents a fundamental shift in how tech companies compete through political spending—and a new category of political ad buyer for campaigns to navigate.

item.title: ICE's call for data is stirring advertisers to push for privacy protections
item.description: ICE is seeking to purchase commercial adtech data for immigration enforcement investigations—prompting industry-wide alarm over data misuse and regulatory backlash. Campaigns must prepare for stricter data handling compliance and potential restrictions on audience data availability.
item.content:encoded: ICE is seeking to purchase commercial adtech data for immigration enforcement investigations—prompting industry-wide alarm over data misuse and regulatory backlash. Campaigns must prepare for stricter data handling compliance and potential restrictions on audience data availability.

item.title: Bionic Adds 66 More Integrations to its Media Buying System—Now 130+ Platforms
item.description: Bionic expanded to 130+ platform integrations via Supermetrics partnership, enabling agencies to centralize media planning data from Amazon DSP, Google DV360, The Trade Desk, Meta, TikTok, YouTube and all major platforms. Automated nightly data pulls eliminate manual spreadsheets. Real-time pacing and optimization mean budget adjustments in hours, not weeks. Competitive advantage: daily performance monitoring.
item.content:encoded: Bionic expanded to 130+ platform integrations via Supermetrics partnership, enabling agencies to centralize media planning data from Amazon DSP, Google DV360, The Trade Desk, Meta, TikTok, YouTube and all major platforms. Automated nightly data pulls eliminate manual spreadsheets. Real-time pacing and optimization mean budget adjustments in hours, not weeks. Competitive advantage: daily performance monitoring.

item.title: Ohio voters will see more AI political content this election cycle
item.description: AI deepfakes are now standard-issue attack ads in Ohio's 2026 primary. Video deepfakes are indistinguishable from authentic content without specialized detection software. Campaigns must plan for AI-generated attack messaging and voter education on spotting fabricated content is becoming a competitive factor. Legal/regulatory framework remains undefined at federal level.

item.title: Steven's Newsletter - May 26, 2026: CTV Upfront Tipping Point and the Reality of Agentic Advertising
item.description: Connected TV upfront dollars now exceed linear TV for the first time—CTV upfronts are the only growing channel. Campaigns without scaled CTV by Q3 face inventory premiums. Autonomous ad buying only works for 10-20% of campaigns; complex political media plans still require human strategists. This is the structural shift separating 2026 winners from laggards.
item.content:encoded: Connected TV upfront dollars now exceed linear TV for the first time—CTV upfronts are the only growing channel. Campaigns without scaled CTV by Q3 face inventory premiums. Autonomous ad buying only works for 10-20% of campaigns; complex political media plans still require human strategists. This is the structural shift separating 2026 winners from laggards.

item.title: California Influencer Disclosures Reveal Hidden Political Spending—$400K Payments with No Disclosure in Federal Races
item.description: Tom Steyer's California gubernatorial campaign paid influencers $10–$400K+ for political promotion with minimal disclosure. Semafor reports $400K to a single creator, $25K payments to accounts with millions of followers. Federal campaigns still have no influencer spending disclosure requirements—2026 will test enforcement and compliance gaps.
item.content:encoded: Tom Steyer's California gubernatorial campaign paid influencers $10–$400K+ for political promotion with minimal disclosure. Semafor reports $400K to a single creator, $25K payments to accounts with millions of followers. Federal campaigns still have no influencer spending disclosure requirements—2026 will test enforcement and compliance gaps.

item.title: Swing Voter Targeting Is Shifting from Demographics to Fandom—Campaigns Are Just Catching Up
item.description: Fandom data (influencer communities, shared identity around creators) is now a primary voter targeting dimension. 21% of Americans get news from social media influencers; fandoms function as built-in distribution networks. Campaigns treating voters as demographics will underperform those activating fandom-based communities.
item.content:encoded: Fandom data (influencer communities, shared identity around creators) is now a primary voter targeting dimension. 21% of Americans get news from social media influencers; fandoms function as built-in distribution networks. Campaigns treating voters as demographics will underperform those activating fandom-based communities.

item.title: Samba TV's Project Gravity Solves the Walled-Garden Problem: Real-Time Identity Across 40+ DSPs
item.description: Samba TV launches Project Gravity—unified voter/consumer identity resolution that activates across 40+ DSPs without platform lock-in. Uses AI vector embedding to match offline voter lists to digital profiles in real-time. Campaigns eliminate data silos and platform-based optimization bias.
item.content:encoded: Samba TV launches Project Gravity—unified voter/consumer identity resolution that activates across 40+ DSPs without platform lock-in. Uses AI vector embedding to match offline voter lists to digital profiles in real-time. Campaigns eliminate data silos and platform-based optimization bias.

item.title: Sabio Q1 2026: Programmatic Revenue Grows 1,174% YoY—Political & Advocacy Commitments Hit $5M+
item.description: Sabio secured $5M+ in political and advocacy commitments as programmatic CTV revenues explode. Core business grew 6% YoY; international and programmatic combined represent 62% of Q1 revenue. Early signal of election cycle acceleration.

item.title: Programmatic Audio Expands Advertiser Reach — The Basis & DAX Partnership Unlocks Voter Targeting at Scale
item.description: Basis and DAX integrate programmatic audio with 60+ political audience datasets—campaigns now activate voter-targeted audio at scale. Audio reaches 80% of adults daily; campaigns ignoring it leave efficiency on the table by Q3 2026.
item.content:encoded: Basis and DAX integrate programmatic audio with 60+ political audience datasets—campaigns now activate voter-targeted audio at scale. Audio reaches 80% of adults daily; campaigns ignoring it leave efficiency on the table by Q3 2026.

item.title: Deep Sync Expands Partnership with MiQ to Power Identity-Driven Political Advertising
item.description: Voter data to digital activation in hours instead of weeks: Deep Sync + MiQ partnership enables campaigns to match voter files to addressable audiences with ~25% addressability improvement on average (~120% on live campaigns), then activate through MiQ programmatic in under 48 hours. Match rates and speed are now competitive advantages.
item.content:encoded: Voter data to digital activation in hours instead of weeks: Deep Sync + MiQ partnership enables campaigns to match voter files to addressable audiences with ~25% addressability improvement on average (~120% on live campaigns), then activate through MiQ programmatic in under 48 hours. Match rates and speed are now competitive advantages.

item.title: How to Evaluate an Advertising Platform for Political Campaigns in 2026
item.description: Platform selection now determines campaign speed and efficiency in 2026—48% of budgets run in final 30 days. Omnichannel activation (CTV, audio, display, social, search) from unified workflows eliminates manual handoffs and saves 51 hours per campaign. Political-specific targeting (congressional districts, voter data integrations) and rapid activation separate winners from laggards.
item.content:encoded: Platform selection now determines campaign speed and efficiency in 2026—48% of budgets run in final 30 days. Omnichannel activation (CTV, audio, display, social, search) from unified workflows eliminates manual handoffs and saves 51 hours per campaign. Political-specific targeting (congressional districts, voter data integrations) and rapid activation separate winners from laggards.

item.title: Video Ad Spending to Set Record for a Midterm Cycle in 2026
item.description: CTV is the only video channel gaining budget share in 2026—projected at 16% growth vs. 2% decline for local broadcast. Campaigns that scale CTV by Q3 avoid October inventory premiums and CPM spikes that nearly double heading into Election Day.
item.content:encoded: CTV is the only video channel gaining budget share in 2026—projected at 16% growth vs. 2% decline for local broadcast. Campaigns that scale CTV by Q3 avoid October inventory premiums and CPM spikes that nearly double heading into Election Day.

item.title: Influencer Spending Exploding in Campaigns—But Compliance Rules Haven't Caught Up
item.description: California's governor race reveals influencer-paid-content gap: campaigns paying $10-$400K per influencer endorsement with vague disclosure rules and no unified federal framework. Influencer networks now core spend category with regulatory risk—FEC/FTC enforcement coming in 2027.
item.content:encoded: California's governor race reveals influencer-paid-content gap: campaigns paying $10-$400K per influencer endorsement with vague disclosure rules and no unified federal framework. Influencer networks now core spend category with regulatory risk—FEC/FTC enforcement coming in 2027.

item.title: LinkedIn Kills "AI Slop"—Volume-Based Content Strategies Now Underperform
item.description: LinkedIn algorithm is actively suppressing AI-generated content without genuine expertise. Authentic thought leadership now wins distribution advantage. Campaigns running volume-based content strategies powered by AI will lose 40-60% reach; strategic pivot to organizer voices required.
item.content:encoded: LinkedIn algorithm is actively suppressing AI-generated content without genuine expertise. Authentic thought leadership now wins distribution advantage. Campaigns running volume-based content strategies powered by AI will lose 40-60% reach; strategic pivot to organizer voices required.

item.title: Federal AI Review Plans Could Reshape Campaign Strategy in 2026
item.description: White House federal AI governance framework will force campaign tech compliance overhaul—mandatory federal review replaces state-by-state patchwork, threatening retroactive compliance costs for existing AI tools. Campaigns must audit vendor roadmaps by Q3 to avoid mid-cycle workflow rebuilds.

item.title: Upfronts 2026: Ad Tech Takes Center Stage
item.description: Streaming platforms (Netflix, Disney, Amazon, Warner Bros.) led 2026 Upfronts with ad-tech, not content. Netflix 60% ad-tier adoption. Amazon reaches 90% of US households with authenticated signals. CTV+AI optimization now structural advantage; campaigns with programmatic precision beat linear-TV-only buyers 2-3x on efficiency.
item.content:encoded: Streaming platforms (Netflix, Disney, Amazon, Warner Bros.) led 2026 Upfronts with ad-tech, not content. Netflix 60% ad-tier adoption. Amazon reaches 90% of US households with authenticated signals. CTV+AI optimization now structural advantage; campaigns with programmatic precision beat linear-TV-only buyers 2-3x on efficiency.

item.title: How Political Advertising Will Impact the Media Landscape in 2026
item.description: CTV is now the ONLY channel growing in political spend ($2.4B vs. $2.34B 2024). Political dollars concentrate in 5 states. Campaigns without early PMP/PG inventory locks by June face $0.12–$0.18 CPM premiums vs. early movers. Deepfake/AI brand safety risk escalating.
item.content:encoded: CTV is now the ONLY channel growing in political spend ($2.4B vs. $2.34B 2024). Political dollars concentrate in 5 states. Campaigns without early PMP/PG inventory locks by June face $0.12–$0.18 CPM premiums vs. early movers. Deepfake/AI brand safety risk escalating.

item.title: Democrats eye 2027 crackdown on AI in election ads
item.description: AI deepfakes are flooding 2026 campaigns with zero disclosure requirements—House Democrats plan 2027 regulation. Smart campaigns disclose voluntary; vendors must build compliance tooling now to retain clients post-election.

item.title: Stop Wasting Ad Dollars: How Social Optimization Changes the Math
item.description: 36.5% of programmatic social spend is wasted on unsuitable content impressions. Leading brands combining contextual suitability with platform filters achieved 29% higher engagement, 71% fewer suitability failures, and 18% lower CPMs. Campaigns spending $10M+ in social can recapture hundreds of thousands in waste by shifting to quality-optimized buying.
item.content:encoded: 36.5% of programmatic social spend is wasted on unsuitable content impressions. Leading brands combining contextual suitability with platform filters achieved 29% higher engagement, 71% fewer suitability failures, and 18% lower CPMs. Campaigns spending $10M+ in social can recapture hundreds of thousands in waste by shifting to quality-optimized buying.

item.title: DoubleVerify Delivers Global Media Quality Measurement for LinkedIn Audience Network
item.description: DoubleVerify brings third-party media quality verification to LinkedIn Audience Network—enabling real-time measurement of invalid traffic, viewability, brand suitability, and geography. LinkedIn LAN delivers 3.9x more impressions than feed-only. Verified, quality-measured inventory is becoming table stakes for political campaigns.
item.content:encoded: DoubleVerify brings third-party media quality verification to LinkedIn Audience Network—enabling real-time measurement of invalid traffic, viewability, brand suitability, and geography. LinkedIn LAN delivers 3.9x more impressions than feed-only. Verified, quality-measured inventory is becoming table stakes for political campaigns.

item.title: Study finds AI chatbots can shape and distort voters' understanding of elections
item.description: Research from UCD and University of Strathclyde reveals AI chatbots (ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini, Grok) provide incomplete, biased, and sometimes fabricated election information. ChatGPT omitted candidates, Gemini showed wrong results. Campaigns must understand this voter information gap affects perception and turnout.

item.title: Broadcast Is Losing Ad Dollars, Despite Massive Reach
item.description: The intelligence infrastructure gap is costing broadcasters $233M in political ad revenue—while programmatic platforms win by proving performance in real-time. Campaigns now demand live data, not quarterly reports. Broadcasters with real-time audience intelligence will win 2026 budgets; those without will lose to faster platforms.
item.content:encoded: The intelligence infrastructure gap is costing broadcasters $233M in political ad revenue—while programmatic platforms win by proving performance in real-time. Campaigns now demand live data, not quarterly reports. Broadcasters with real-time audience intelligence will win 2026 budgets; those without will lose to faster platforms.

item.title: Why Political Advertisers Can't Afford Unverified Reach In 2026
item.description: Probabilistic identity doesn't scale for political campaigns operating 30-day windows with FEC disclosure requirements. Unverified reach introduces compliance risk, performance waste, and slower tactical optimization. Authenticated inventory commands premium but ensures certainty and campaign velocity.

item.title: Ampersand Unveils TrueStream Political: Authenticated CTV Inventory at Scale
item.description: Ampersand launches TrueStream Political with deterministic household-level identity from Comcast, Charter, Cox (120M households). Fraud-free premium inventory addresses identity accuracy variance crisis (20-30% variance across platforms) plaguing CTV political buys.

item.title: Viant and Ad Fontes Media Bring Political Bias-Based Targeting to CTV News
item.description: First DSP enabling news reliability-based targeting in CTV. Campaigns can now target voters based on news environment consumed (mainstream, conservative, entertainment-aligned). Reach swing voters in mainstream environments or base voters in conservative environments with tailored messaging.

item.title: Digital Remedy Expands Political Media Offering With Launch Of The Political Desk
item.description: Digital Remedy launches The Political Desk with VoterReach district-level measurement. Campaigns get unified view across CTV/video/display/audio/DOOH plus real-time frequency detection and budget reallocation. Launches in hours, not days—critical speed advantage for 2026.

item.title: Local TV faces financial pinch as political ad spending increasingly moves online
item.description: Broadcast TV political spend drops 7% while online jumps 35%; online now captures 50% of all political ad spend for first time in midterm cycle. Campaigns must shift allocation by Q3 or face premium CPM rates and shrinking inventory.

item.title: A new generation of ads for the AI era of Search
item.description: Google launched Conversational Discovery ads and Highlighted Answers powered by Gemini in Search—allowing campaigns to generate custom product explainers in real-time based on voter queries. Political media buyers gain new demand-capture inventory at scale.
item.content:encoded: Google launched Conversational Discovery ads and Highlighted Answers powered by Gemini in Search—allowing campaigns to generate custom product explainers in real-time based on voter queries. Political media buyers gain new demand-capture inventory at scale.

item.title: How AIPAC channels millions through shell PACs ahead of US elections
item.description: Shell PAC structures are proliferating as major groups hide spending sources—layered PAC-to-PAC funding is becoming the primary strategy to obscure donor origins. Political operatives expect increased transparency pressure and FEC enforcement scrutiny on opaque funding chains.

item.title: Democrats eye 2027 crackdown on AI in election ads
item.description: AI-generated political ads are proliferating without disclosure—Democrats plan mandatory AI disclaimer legislation if they retake power. Campaigns must prepare compliance frameworks and audit trails for AI-created content by 2027 or face regulatory exposure.

item.title: Can you trust that post about Tom Steyer? How paid influencers are flooding into the governor's race
item.description: Billionaire Steyer's campaign pays influencers $123K+ to post pro-campaign content; some posts lack required disclosure. Signals influencer content at scale becoming standard political tactic, but with disclosure/transparency vulnerabilities. Campaigns testing paid creator networks to build grassroots appearance.
item.content:encoded: Billionaire Steyer's campaign pays influencers $123K+ to post pro-campaign content; some posts lack required disclosure. Signals influencer content at scale becoming standard political tactic, but with disclosure/transparency vulnerabilities. Campaigns testing paid creator networks to build grassroots appearance.

item.title: U.S. Digital Video Ad Spend Projected to Surpass $80B in 2026, Growing 11% YoY
item.description: Digital video ad spending (CTV, social video, YouTube) hits $81.9B in 2026, growing 11% YoY—nearly 20% faster than overall ad market. Political campaigns must prioritize video channels where growth outpaces traditional media; CTV and streaming now mandatory for efficient reach.
item.content:encoded: Digital video ad spending (CTV, social video, YouTube) hits $81.9B in 2026, growing 11% YoY—nearly 20% faster than overall ad market. Political campaigns must prioritize video channels where growth outpaces traditional media; CTV and streaming now mandatory for efficient reach.

item.title: Midterm Political Ad Spend Projected To Hit $13.8B, Up 9%
item.description: 2026 political ad spend reaches $13.8B (+9% vs 2022), but digital now commands 50% of budget (up from 46%), while local TV falls to 36% (from 40%). This marks structural shift away from traditional broadcast toward digital, social, and programmatic channels.
item.content:encoded: 2026 political ad spend reaches $13.8B (+9% vs 2022), but digital now commands 50% of budget (up from 46%), while local TV falls to 36% (from 40%). This marks structural shift away from traditional broadcast toward digital, social, and programmatic channels.

item.title: Trump got his revenge on Thomas Massie — but at a huge price
item.description: Kentucky's 4th House primary becomes most expensive in U.S. history at $32.6M, with $14M from outside groups. Signals unprecedented primary-cycle spending arms races; campaigns must prepare for massive GOP internal battles funded by Super PACs and Trump-aligned organizations.
